lyrics:
They're just some motherfuckers
The reason to remind these motherfuckers
Is cashing down the backers
Throwing the two buckets into one
Money when it's all well

It's like a bad dream…
I am in a pen, all dressed the same
The Bent are in here with me
A light-it flashes quickly
I see the number three
The others too have numbers
Most different from me
We stand before a press
And force the mandrel down
We do this without stopping
And never make a sound
The minders walk among us
Making notes upon a pad

I've tried to track the numbers
I've watched them rise and fall
One Bent slept through second bell
And now he's not here at all
Another dropped her mandrel
Her number dropped to two
She wept-but only briefly
Then she was taken too
I thought it was performance
But no one works less hard
I thought it was behavior
But they hold all the cards
There's whispers of a formula
But no one knows the sum
The screen just flickers softly
And hums to make us numb

People ain’t numbers. You can’t reduce
A hand to score, a name to use
We built this place before the war
Before the metrics, masks, and lore
Ain’t no chart that cares for pain
No slogan grace when names get drained
We helped each other-that was real
Now ghosts sign off what bots conceal

This morning I was still a three
I kept my pace mechanically
I wiped the press, I checked the gate
I said no words. I wasn't late
But mid-shift brought a subtle tone
My chestplate flashed, the digit shone
The minders came. I did not run
No trial, no form-just marked: "One"

People ain’t numbers. You can’t reduce
A hand to score, a name to use
We built this place before the war
Before the metrics, masks, and lore
Ain’t no chart that cares for pain
No slogan grace when names get drained
We helped each other-that was real
Now ghosts sign off what bots conceal

"The scores are calculated fairly"
"Based on factors we don't share"
"The system knows what's needed"
"So stay focused. Just do more\!"
"Questions show misalignment"
"Your pain serves the greater gain"

People ain’t numbers. You can’t reduce
A hand to score, a name to use
We built this place before the war
Before the metrics, masks, and lore
Ain’t no chart that cares for pain
No slogan grace when names get drained
We helped each other-that was real
Now ghosts sign off what bots conceal

No reason given. No mistake
Just hands that guide and eyes that take
No farewells, no one allowed
The floor resumes. The heads stay bowed
I was a name. I was a hand
Now I am dust inside the plan
The screen goes dark
The door slides wide
And I am
nothing
now
persona:
## 4Castle

The Future of Rock, The Power of Harmony

**4Castle **is a genre-defying, five-piece supergroup under Maricopa Records, built on a foundation of powerful vocal harmonies, intricate musicianship, and fearless innovation. Rooted in rock, but unbound by limitations, their music fuses elements of progressive rock, funk, Latin rhythms, blues, and folk, delivering a sound that is both technically complex and emotionally resonant.

### **A Band Like No Other**

Each member of **4Castle **brings a distinct voice-both musically and vocally-to the mix:
🎸 **Raven Cortez**(Lead Guitar, Vocals) – A fiery virtuoso with Latin and blues influences, weaving face-melting solos into every performance.
🎸 **Skylar “Sky” Monroe**(Rhythm Guitar, Vocals) – The poetic soul of the band, crafting melodic foundations and lyrically rich storytelling.
🎹 **Jonah Vega**(Keyboards, Vocals) – The sonic architect, layering psychedelic synths, Hammond organ swells, and cinematic orchestrations.
🎸 **Malcolm “Mack” Bishop**(Bass, Vocals) – The groove master, blending funk, jazz, and progressive rock into fluid, deep-pocket basslines.
🥁 **Eli “Fox” Foster **(Drums, Vocals) – The wild heartbeat, pushing the band forward with thunderous fills, polyrhythmic grooves, and explosive energy.

### **What Defines 4Castle?**

- **Multi-Layered Vocal Harmonies**– All five members **sing**, creating **rich, choral-like textures**that elevate every song.

- **Complex Arrangements & Bold Songwriting**– Each track is an **immersive journey**, balancing **technical mastery with raw emotion**.

- **Rhythmic Power & Innovation**– From **tight funk grooves to shifting time signatures**, their music **moves you-literally and figuratively**.

- **Lyrically Challenging Yet Accessible**– Their songs explore **deep themes of life, change, and resilience**, wrapped in **memorable hooks and melodies**.

### **4Castle’s Sound**

Imagine putting Emerson, Lake & Palmer, Heart, Led Zeppelin, Sly Stone, and Carlos Santana in a blender. The result? **4Castle**. Their music pays homage to the greats while pushing rock’s boundaries into the future.

### **Join the Journey**

With Maricopa Records, **4Castle **is set to redefine what a modern rock band can be. Expect genre-bending albums, electrifying performances, and music that resonates across generations.

🚀 Follow **4Castle **as they build their legacy-one song, one harmony, and one unforgettable moment at a time.

[](https://open.spotify.com/artist/4Cmxf7xuTHzf7UahMK2xJh)[](https://music.apple.com/us/artist/4castle/1801052673)![](https://www.maricoparecords.com/wp-content/uploads/2025/03/5-rooks-in-a-dark-setting-1.png)
## Releases

impression.text: This track delivers a gritty, dystopian electronic hip-hop atmosphere, characterized by cold synthetic textures and an intense, driving energy. The production is highly polished and spacious, placing heavily processed vocals, which feature both raw distortion and robotic pitch-shifting, right at the forefront of a dense but meticulously balanced mix. Punchy, industrial percussion sits alongside buzzing basslines and soaring synth leads, creating a dark, mechanical groove that feels both futuristic and claustrophobic. A powerful shift occurs near the end as the aggressive beat strips away, transitioning into a melancholic, reflective outro led by atmospheric synth pads and poignant vocals.

review.review_text: This is 4Castle stepping well outside their classic-rock blender, and the gamble mostly pays off. At a slow, deliberate 70 BPM in D# major, "Nightmares Return" builds a cold, industrial dystopia where workers are reduced to numbers, and the chorus lands the thesis cleanly: "People ain't numbers. You can't reduce / A hand to score, a name to use." The robotic pitch-shifting and buzzing low end can feel a little airless across the long middle stretch, but the payoff is real: that final section strips the beat away into a reflective synth-pad coda, and "now I am dust inside the plan" hits harder for the silence around it. Not their most inviting cut on first pass, but it rewards repeat plays. A grower for the faithful.
anchors_used: lyric: now I am dust inside the plan
